Art collecting involves acquiring, preserving, and displaying artworks for aesthetic or financial purposes. It requires an understanding of art market trends, artist values, and collector goals.

This question requires an understanding of these complexities, as well as the various tools and strategies collectors use to evaluate artworks and make informed purchasing decisions. Climate change poses significant challenges for art collections worldwide, including rising temperatures, increased humidity, and new conservation priorities. This question requires an understanding of these impacts and the various strategies collectors use to mitigate them. A beginner-friendly answer might acknowledge the importance of ensuring proper climate control in storage facilities, while a more in-depth explanation might involve discussing specific conservation techniques and materials.
